kyc.description: Know Your Customer — identity verification required before financial transactions
kyc.minimum: Full name, DOB, address, government ID
kyc.enhanced: Enhanced due diligence for high-risk customers or large transactions

aml.description: Anti-Money Laundering — transaction monitoring for suspicious patterns
aml.requirements: OFAC screening, SAR filing for suspicious activity > $5,000 USD

pci_dss.description: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
pci_dss.rule: Never store raw card numbers — use a payment processor tokenization (Stripe, Adyen)

gdpr_ccpa.description: Right to erasure applies to financial data — must be able to delete personal data
gdpr_ccpa.exception: Regulatory records (7 years) cannot be deleted — use pseudonymization

money_transmission.description: Transmitting money on behalf of users requires state/national licenses
money_transmission.path: Use a regulated BaaS partner (Unit, Column, Stripe Treasury) to avoid direct licensing

security_requirements: All financial data encrypted at rest and in transit (AES-256, TLS 1.2+)
PCI DSS compliance — never store raw card numbers, use tokenization
Multi-factor authentication for all account access
Fraud detection on every transaction (velocity checks, device fingerprinting)
Audit log of every financial operation with timestamps and actor IDs
Soft deletes with 7-year retention for regulatory compliance
IP allowlisting for admin and internal tooling
Penetration testing before launch and annually
best_practices: Partner with a regulated BaaS provider (Unit, Column, Stripe Treasury) before building core banking
Build fraud detection before going live — not as an afterthought
Design for disputes and chargebacks from day one — they will happen
Never store raw card numbers or bank account numbers
Use webhooks for payment status — polling creates race conditions
Implement idempotency keys on all payment mutations
Test every payment flow with declined cards and network timeouts
Display clear fee schedules — hidden fees cause chargeback spikes
common_risks: Regulatory non-compliance — operating without required licenses
Fraud at scale — losses can exceed revenue without proper detection
Chargeback spiral — high chargeback rates lead to account termination by Stripe/processors
Bank partner concentration risk — single banking partner means single point of failure
Data breach — financial PII (SSN, account numbers) is highest-value breach target
Operational errors in ledger — double charges, missing credits destroy user trust instantly
recommended_integrations: Stripe — card payments, payouts, subscriptions, identity verification
Plaid — bank account linking and ACH transfers
Unit or Column — BaaS for bank accounts, cards, and ACH
Resend — transaction confirmation emails, suspicious activity alerts
